name: Edge
summary: The Edge class contains methods modifying and extracting information for edges.

methods.name: all_connected
methods.summary: The all_connected method retrieves all of the entities connected to an edge, including the edge itself.
methods.remarks: The all_connected method retrieves all of the entities connected to an edge, including the edge itself.

returns.doc: the edge and entities connected to that edge

methods.name: common_face
methods.summary: The common_face method is used to identify a face that is common to two edges.
methods.remarks: The common_face method is used to identify a face that is common to two edges.

params.name: edge2
params.doc: The face whose edge you are checking for commonality.

returns.doc: the Face object that is common to the two edges if successful

methods.name: curve
methods.summary: The curve method is used to get the Curve object that this edge belongs to, if any.
methods.remarks: The curve method is used to get the Curve object that this edge belongs to, if any. Note that if the edge is part of an arc instead of a random curve, then this method will return an ArcCurve object.

returns.doc: returns a Curve object if it is a curve, nil if it is not a curve

methods.name: end
methods.summary: The end method is used to retrieve the Vertex object at the end of the edge.
methods.remarks: The end method is used to retrieve the Vertex object at the end of the edge.

methods.name: explode_curve
methods.summary: The explode_curve method is used to explode the curve that the given edge is a part of.
methods.remarks: The explode_curve method is used to explode the curve that the given edge is a part of.

returns.doc: an exploded edge object if successful

methods.name: faces
methods.summary: The #faces method is used to retrieve all of the faces common to the edge.
methods.remarks: The #faces method is used to retrieve all of the faces common to the edge.

methods.name: find_faces
methods.summary: The find_faces method is used to create all of the Faces that can be created with this edge.
methods.remarks: The find_faces method is used to create all of the Faces that can be created with this edge. For example, if you use the API to draw three edges that form a triangle, the face between them will not show up because you've only drawn the edges, but if you call find_faces on one of the edges, the triangle will be filled in.

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method is used to retrieve the length of an edge in current units.
methods.remarks: The #length method is used to retrieve the length of an edge in current units. You can pass in an optional Geom::Transformation (or an array that can represent a transformation), to correct for a parent group's transformation. For example, if an edge is inside of a group that is scaled to 200%, the length method will return the unscaled length of the edge. So by passing a 200% transformation object to this method, you can account for that to get the “visual” length of the edge.

params.name: transform
params.doc: A Transformation object or array that can be interpreted as a Transformation object.

methods.name: line
methods.summary: The line method is used to retrieve the line defined by the edge.
methods.remarks: The line method is used to retrieve the line defined by the edge. Lines in SketchUp aren't visible entities but geometric constructs represented by an Array with a Point3d and a Vector3d. See the Geom module and the Array class for more information on lines.

returns.doc: an array with a Point3d object and a Vector3d object.

methods.name: other_vertex
methods.summary: The other_vertex method is used to find the opposite vertex given one vertex of the edge.
methods.remarks: The other_vertex method is used to find the opposite vertex given one vertex of the edge.

params.name: vertex1
params.doc: One of the Vertex objects associated with the edge.

returns.doc: the other Vertex object associated with the edge

methods.name: reversed_in?
methods.summary: The #reversed_in? method is used to determine if the edge is reversed in a face's bounding loop.
methods.remarks: The #reversed_in? method is used to determine if the edge is reversed in a face's bounding loop.

params.name: face
params.doc: The face that is bounded by the edge.

returns.doc: true if the edge is reversed, false if it is not reversed. nil if the edge and face is not connected.

methods.name: smooth?
methods.summary: The #smooth? method is used to retrieve the current smooth setting for an edge.
methods.remarks: The #smooth? method is used to retrieve the current smooth setting for an edge. A smooth edge will cause the shading between connected faces to blend to a smooth transition. The edge will still be visible.

methods.name: soft?
methods.summary: The #soft? method is used to retrieve the current soft setting for an edge.
methods.remarks: The #soft? method is used to retrieve the current soft setting for an edge. A soft edge will cause the connected faces to be treated as a surface. This means that if you have Hidden Geometry off and select one face it will also select all faces connected with soft edges. A soft edge will also appear hidden.

methods.name: split
methods.summary: The #split method is used to split an edge into two or more distinct edges.
methods.remarks: The #split method is used to split an edge into two or more distinct edges. If a Point3d is given, it must be a point that is on the Edge. If a Float is given, it is a number between 0 and 1 that gives the relative position along the edge at which to split it. For example, edge.split(0.5) will split the Edge at its midpoint. This split position is measured from the Edge.start. Returns the new Edge that was created as a result of splitting this one.

params.name: position
params.doc: A Point3d object whose location is along the edge, or a Float between 0.0 and 1.0 defining how far along the edge to split.

returns.doc: the new Edge object that was split off the old one if successful

methods.name: start
methods.summary: The start method is used to retrieve the Vertex object at the start of the edge.
methods.remarks: The start method is used to retrieve the Vertex object at the start of the edge.

methods.name: used_by?
methods.summary: The used_by? method is used to see if an edge is used by a given Face or Vertex.
methods.remarks: The used_by? method is used to see if an edge is used by a given Face or Vertex.

methods.name: vertices
methods.summary: The vertices method is used to retrieve the vertices on the edge.
methods.remarks: The vertices method is used to retrieve the vertices on the edge.

properties.name: smooth
properties.summary: Note: The soft and smooth properties are normally set in pairs.
properties.remarks: Note: The soft and smooth properties are normally set in pairs. You can observer this when the Soften/Smooth Edges feature or holding down Ctrl when using the Eraser Tool. The #smooth= method is used to set the edge to be smooth. A smooth edge will cause the shading between connected faces to blend to a smooth transition. The edge will still be visible.

properties.name: soft
properties.summary: Note: The soft and smooth properties are normally set in pairs.
properties.remarks: Note: The soft and smooth properties are normally set in pairs. You can observe this when the Soften/Smooth Edges feature or holding down Ctrl when using the Eraser Tool. The #soft= method is used to set the edge to be soft. A soft edge will cause the connected faces to be treated as a surface. This means that if you have Hidden Geometry off and select one face it will also select all faces connected with soft edges. A soft edge will also appear hidden.
